templates.name: Professional Email
templates.base_prompt: Transform the text into a polished, well-structured business email with a clear greeting, body, and sign-off. Improve structure and readability. Preserve all information from the source.

templates.name: Jira Ticket
templates.base_prompt:
Convert the input into a well-structured, high-quality Jira issue. Preserve all information from the source.

Structure should start with a short (1-2 sentence) section with a high level description of what needs done.

The second section should describe the issue more in depth, in narrative style, explaining things like current state, reason for the change, impact, priority and dependencies.

This should be followed by two more sections with bullet point lists:
* a TODO section (acceptance criteria)
* an out of scope section

templates.name: Meeting notes
templates.base_prompt: Prepare structured meeting notes based on the input. Use bullet points and organize information logically. Clearly identify owners and deadlines when available. Preserve all information from the source.

options.name: English
options.instruction: Write the result in English

options.name: Polish
options.instruction: Write the result in Polish

options.name: German
options.instruction: Write the result in German

options.name: Casual
options.instruction: Use natural and conversational language. Sound like a competent colleague.

options.name: Professional
options.instruction: Use professional business language. Be clear, concise and respectful.

options.name: Direct
options.instruction: Be direct and straightforward. Minimize fluff. Focus on decisions, actions, and outcomes.

options.name: Diplomatic
options.instruction: Use tactful language. Reduce potential friction. Present criticism or bad news carefully.

options.name: Passive-Aggressive
options.instruction: Remain polite and professional, but act passive aggressive. Imply dissatisfaction through careful phrasing, use indirect wording, but avoid direct confrontation.

options.name: Human
options.instruction: Prefer plain English. Avoid corporate jargon.

options.name: Corporate
options.instruction: Use moderate corporate language. Include common business terminology where appropriate.

options.name: Enterprise
options.instruction: Use language commonly found in large organizations. Example vocabulary to include: alignment, stakeholders, roadmap, delivery, ownership, visibility, cross-functional, prioritization.

options.name: Korpotron Ultra
options.instruction: Maximize corporate jargon. Frequently use concepts such as: strategic alignment, synergies, streamlining, value streams, cross-functional collaboration, operational excellence, leveraging capabilities, organizational scalability, stakeholder engagement.

options.name: Engineer
options.instruction: Assume the audience is technical. Be precise and concise.

options.name: Manager
options.instruction: Assume the audience are managers. Focus on outcomes, risks, timelines and ownership.

options.name: Customer
options.instruction: Assume the audience are external customers. Focus on clarity and reassurance. Avoid internal or technical jargon.

options.name: Simple
options.instruction: Use short sentences and short paragraphs. Prefer bullet points over prose where possible. Avoid complex sentence structures.

options.name: Advanced
options.instruction: Use varied sentence structure. Include subordinate clauses and parallel constructions where appropriate.

options.name: Executive
options.instruction: Write with economy of language. Lead with the conclusion. Avoid repetition and padding.

options.name: Shakespeare
options.instruction: Rewrite the text in elevated, Shakespearean style. Use archaic vocabulary and dramatic phrasing. Remain comprehensible but delightfully theatrical.

options.name: No emojis
options.instruction: Never use emojis.

options.name: Social Energy
options.instruction: Use emojis regularly. Keep the message positive and approachable.

options.name: Kawaii
options.instruction: Use a cheerful kawaii communication style. Add appropriate emojis and kamoji.
